description: Add the fff file-search toolkit to the Pi harness via the @ff-labs/pi-fff Pi extension, pinned in .pi/settings.json (same mechanism as pi-autoresearch/pi-dynamic-workflows). Adds ffgrep/fffind tools + frecency-ranked @-mention autocomplete, a soft conditional prompt hint, a docs page, an agent-readable wiki entry, and a CHANGELOG bullet. Pi-only; no MCP-server route, no override mode, no other-harness wiring.

userStories.title: Pin pi-fff and keep the settings test green
userStories.description: As the Pi harness, I want @ff-labs/pi-fff@0.9.5 pinned in .pi/settings.json so the sandbox installs fff's file-search tools on startup.
acceptanceCriteria: .pi/settings.json packages[] contains "npm:@ff-labs/pi-fff@0.9.5", inserted as the last npm: pin (after npm:pi-autoresearch@1.6.0, before the trailing git: pi-dynamic-workflows pin).
0.9.5 is confirmed to still be the latest dist-tag at impl time (npm view @ff-labs/pi-fff dist-tags.latest).
.pi/extensions/__tests__/settings.test.ts toEqual array is updated to the new exact array in the same committed order.
git diff .pi/settings.json shows exactly one added line; no other pin reordered or bumped.
npx vitest run .pi/extensions/__tests__/settings.test.ts passes.
Tests pass.
userStories.notes: Completed 2026-06-19 (ship-spec inline impl); settings.json +1 line, settings.test.ts updated, vitest green.

userStories.title: Add a soft conditional fff prompt hint
userStories.description: As an operator, I want the Pi agent to prefer fff tools for file search when they are available, without overriding native tools.
acceptanceCriteria: .pi/APPEND_SYSTEM.md gains exactly one short section (<= 6 lines) preferring ffgrep/fffind for file search/grep WHEN those tools are available.
The hint does not instruct the agent to disable, remove, or override native tools (preference order only, not PI_FFF_MODE=override).
Existing Project context and Slack Bridge Awareness sections are preserved verbatim.
Tests pass.
userStories.notes: Completed 2026-06-19 (ship-spec inline impl); one 'File search' section (3 lines), 'when available' guard, native tools kept as fallback.

userStories.title: Document the integration and record the change
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I want docs/integrations/pi-fff.md + a CHANGELOG entry so the integration is discoverable and follows the existing Pi-package doc pattern.
acceptanceCriteria: docs/integrations/pi-fff.md exists with frontmatter incl. sidebar_position: 4, mirroring pi-autoresearch.md (intro + pin snippet, What it adds incl. /fff-mode, verify section, Disable/Remove section, troubleshooting table).
The doc records that @ff-labs/fff-node ships prebuilt binaries (no Rust toolchain) and notes the resolved fff-node version + resolution date (2026-06-19).

Troubleshooting table has rows for (a) fff tools not registered / native binding install failed (network or libc mismatch) and (b) empty @-mention autocomplete on first launch (frecency warms over use; /fff-rescan).
CHANGELOG.md gains one [Unreleased] bullet following the existing ([#NNN](.../issues/NNN)) convention using issue #261.
pnpm run lint passes; root vitest suite passes.
Tests pass.
userStories.notes: Completed 2026-06-19 (ship-spec inline impl); docs/integrations/pi-fff.md (sidebar_position 4, Disable + 4-row troubleshooting), CHANGELOG #261 bullet.

userStories.title: Add an agent-readable wiki entry
userStories.description: As a future agent, I want wiki/pi-fff.md so the always-on fff behavior and the prompt-hint preference are loadable into agent context via /wiki-query.
acceptanceCriteria: wiki/raw/2026-06-19-pi-fff.md immutable snapshot of the upstream README Pi section captured as the provenance source.

wiki/pi-fff.md follows context/rules/wiki.md schema: frontmatter (title, slug: pi-fff, tags, created/updated 2026-06-19, sources -> the raw snapshot, confidence: provisional), ## Summary, ## Detail (always-on tools + autocomplete + prompt hint, with .pi/settings.json and .pi/APPEND_SYSTEM.md cites), ## See Also; <= 600 words.
wiki/README.md index is refreshed.
bash evals/probes/wiki-readme-index.sh passes.
Tests pass.
userStories.notes: Both critics flagged fff is always-on + agent-facing -> wiki (agent-readable), not just docs (human-only).
